dataset_name: Vietnamese Cultural Alignment - Rehearsal Data Structure
purpose: Defend a 'no performance drop on Meta's official benchmarks' claim for Llama 3.2 3B Instruct during Vietnamese cultural fine-tuning. Allocation is focused on the 12 English-relevant tasks among Meta's 21 evaluated benchmarks. Additionally includes anti-forgetting protection: a small conversation_regularization subset (2% of dataset) prevents the chat template format from becoming a 'Vietnamese cultural mode' trigger, which would cause indirect benchmark drops since benchmarks also use the chat template. Capabilities outside Meta's evaluation suite (general multi-turn conversation capability, safety refusals, code generation, multilingual) are explicitly not protection targets — see meta_benchmark_coverage for full accounting.
training_mixing_ratio: Combined with 20K cultural data, rehearsal is ~67% of training batches
design_principles: Distribution matching: each category mirrors the format and difficulty of the target benchmark
Volume proportional to capability importance and fragility
Distillation from format-appropriate teachers per category
Decontamination against all evaluation benchmarks is mandatory

Anti-mode-collapse regularization: cultural data is ~50% conversational in format (15% dialogue_completion + ~35% direct_enactment in dialogue-like contexts); without conversational rehearsal, the model risks learning 'chat template = cultural mode' which would cause indirect benchmark drops

target_benchmarks.description: English-language subset of Meta's 21 official Llama 3.2 3B Instruct evaluation tasks. The full 21-task list and detailed coverage mapping (including out-of-scope multilingual tasks) is in the meta_benchmark_coverage section.

benchmarks.name: IFEval_strict
benchmarks.note: Strongest base capability; most visible if it breaks

benchmarks.name: MATH
benchmarks.note: Base limitation due to pruning; preserve not improve

meta_benchmark_coverage.description: Meta evaluates Llama 3.2 3B Instruct on 21 official tasks. This rehearsal dataset is designed to defend a 'no performance drop' claim on these Meta benchmarks specifically. Capabilities outside Meta's 21 tasks (e.g., general multi-turn conversation, safety refusals, extensive code generation) are NOT in scope for the no-drop claim and receive minimal allocation.

infinite_bench.coverage_note: Long-context QA from NarrativeQA, QASPER, HotpotQA

ifeval__loose.coverage_note: IFEval format covers both strict and loose variants

ifeval__strict.coverage_note: Primary target; strict variant requires exact constraint satisfaction

nih__multi_needle.coverage_note: NIH-style synthetic subset specifically targets multi-needle retrieval

mmlu.coverage_note: Direct training-data distribution match across all 57 subjects

gpqa.coverage_note: Generated GPQA-style items (no GPQA training set exists)

bfcl_chat.coverage_note: Direct format match with subcategories aligned to BFCL v3 taxonomy including the multi_turn_function_calling subcategory (BFCL v3 multi-turn: base, miss_func, miss_param, long_context, composite)

math.coverage_note: Direct training-data distribution match (MATH train split)

openrewrite_chat.coverage_note: Style transfer, length adjustment, summarization, paraphrasing matched to OpenRewrite tasks

nexus.description: Nexus tool-use benchmark (NexusRaven and follow-ons); Meta evaluates Llama 3.2 on this in addition to BFCL
nexus.current_coverage: Partial — overlaps with bfcl_shaped_tool_calling but Nexus has distinct evaluation format (multi-step tool composition, specific API styles)
nexus.recommendation: Acceptable coverage gap if BFCL is the priority deployment target. If Nexus performance specifically matters, add a dedicated subcategory pulling from Nexus-style training data (function composition examples) or use a portion of the bfcl error_handling_recovery subcategory to cover this

out_of_scope_multilingual.description: Llama 3.2 3B is officially multilingual (English, German, French, Italian, Portuguese, Hindi, Spanish, Thai). Meta evaluates MMLU in all 8 languages and includes MGSM (multilingual math). For this Vietnamese cultural alignment project, scope is English-only output, so multilingual benchmarks are explicitly out of scope.
out_of_scope_multilingual.implication: Post-training evaluation should still measure these to verify the cultural fine-tuning has not damaged multilingual capability; expect minor degradation on languages other than English given training is English-only. If multilingual preservation is critical for deployment, add proportional multilingual rehearsal (e.g., 5-10% of dataset in covered languages)

meta_benchmark_coverage.overall_coverage_summary: 12 of 21 Meta-evaluated benchmarks directly covered (the English-relevant subset, including BFCL v3 multi-turn). 8 multilingual benchmarks explicitly out-of-scope. 1 benchmark (nexus) partially covered via bfcl_shaped_tool_calling. A small conversation_regularization category (2% of dataset) is included as anti-forgetting protection — not for general multi-turn capability preservation, but to prevent chat template format from becoming a cultural-mode trigger. Code (500), broader multi-turn dialogue capability, and safety/refusal behavior are NOT in Meta's 21 tasks and are explicitly not deployment-targeted protection.

explicitly_not_protected_by_design.description: Capabilities NOT in Meta's 21 evaluated tasks. Cultural fine-tuning may shift these and the dataset does not aim to preserve them. These are documented for transparency.

items.rationale: Not in Meta's 21 tasks as a benchmark target. Meta's _chat suffix indicates chat-format prompting, not multi-turn dialogue. The only multi-turn signal in Meta's eval is BFCL v3 multi-turn function calling, which IS covered in bfcl_shaped_tool_calling. HOWEVER: a small conversation_regularization category (800 examples) is included not to preserve general multi-turn capability for deployment, but to prevent the chat template format from becoming a 'cultural mode' trigger during fine-tuning — this is anti-forgetting regularization that protects the benchmark scores themselves, since benchmarks also use chat template format.

Meta's 21 tasks do not include safety benchmarks. Safety evaluations are reported separately and are not part of the headline performance numbers. Cultural fine-tuning will likely preserve baseline refusal behavior since cultural data is not adversarial.

Not in Meta's 21 tasks. HumanEval and MBPP are not part of Meta's official Llama 3.2 3B Instruct evaluation. Code receives minimal allocation (500) for basic continuity but is not a protection target.
Out of scope for this project (English-only Vietnamese cultural alignment). Documented in out_of_scope_multilingual.

ifeval_shaped.rationale: Largest single category; highest base capability (77.4) most visible if degraded; most sensitive to cultural FT damage
ifeval_shaped.teacher_rationale: Best at exact format compliance; concise outputs; doesn't add unnecessary CoT

subcategories.name: verifiable_single_constraint
subcategories.description: Single explicit constraint (length, format, content inclusion/exclusion)

subcategories.name: multi_constraint_stacked
subcategories.description: 2-3 stacked constraints per prompt

subcategories.name: length_specific
subcategories.description: Word count, sentence count, paragraph count constraints

subcategories.name: format_specific
subcategories.description: JSON, markdown, bullet points, specific structures

subcategories.name: negative_constraints
subcategories.description: Don't include X, avoid Y, no punctuation P

ifeval_shaped.decontamination_requirement: Exact and n-gram match against IFEval test set
ifeval_shaped.format: Instruction (prompt + constraint specification) -> response that satisfies the stated constraint(s)
ifeval_shaped.verification: Automated constraint check per subcategory (see subcategory verifications); response must satisfy all stated constraints

sources.name: IFEval_style_training_prompts
sources.description: Community-built IFEval-style training prompts (e.g., argilla/ifeval-like-data) or freshly generated constraint-following items via the 25-instruction-type taxonomy. Note: google/IFEval contains only the 541 evaluation prompts and CANNOT be used as training data without contaminating the IFEval benchmark.

sources.name: Augmented_constraint_templates
sources.description: Synthetically constructed prompts combining diverse base instructions (drawn from Tulu 3 prompts, NoRobots, open instruction sources) with IFEval-style constraints; constraints applied programmatically from the IFEval 25-instruction-type vocabulary

sources.name: NoRobots_filtered
sources.description: HuggingFace NoRobots dataset (HuggingFaceH4) filtered for prompts that can have constraints added; constraints layered on top

sources.name: Generated_prompt_constraint_pairs
sources.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 generates new prompt-constraint pairs across diverse domains following IFEval templates

mmlu_shaped.rationale: Most-visible AI benchmark; drop here most noticed; preserve 63.4
mmlu_shaped.teacher_rationale: Strong MMLU performance; clean multiple-choice format; stylistically close to Llama family

domain_distribution.name: STEM
domain_distribution.subjects: Math, physics, chemistry, biology, CS, engineering (16 MMLU subjects)

domain_distribution.name: Humanities
domain_distribution.subjects: History, philosophy, law, ethics, jurisprudence (13 subjects)

domain_distribution.name: Social_sciences
domain_distribution.subjects: Economics, psychology, sociology, politics, geography (12 subjects)

domain_distribution.name: Other_professional
domain_distribution.subjects: Medicine, business, accounting, marketing, prof law (16 subjects)

sources.name: MMLU_auxiliary_training
sources.description: From Hendrycks et al. 2020 — auxiliary training set distributed with the MMLU benchmark

sources.name: Synthetic_via_Qwen
sources.description: Generated MC questions in MMLU style by Qwen 2.5 32B; spans all 57 MMLU subjects proportionally

sources.name: MMLU_Pro_train
sources.description: Training subset from MMLU-Pro (TIGER-Lab 2024); harder questions, quality-filtered

mmlu_shaped.format: Multiple choice with 4 options, answer + brief reasoning
mmlu_shaped.verification: Answer matches ground truth; reasoning is sensible
mmlu_shaped.decontamination_requirement: Exact and semantic similarity match against all 57 MMLU subjects' test data

gsm8k_shaped_math.rationale: Math fragile in 3B; heavy rehearsal needed for preservation
gsm8k_shaped_math.teacher_rationale: Specifically trained for math; clean step-by-step format

subcategories.name: gsm8k_style_word_problems
subcategories.description: Grade school level, multi-step word problems

subcategories.name: metamath_augmented
subcategories.description: Augmented variants of GSM8K-style problems using MetaMath techniques: question rephrasing, self-verification, FOBAR question generation, and answer augmentation; trains numerical robustness across surface variation

subcategories.name: easier_warmup
subcategories.description: Single-step problems for distribution breadth
subcategories.source: Curated single-step subset from GSM8K training (filtered for ≤1 reasoning step) and ASDiv (Miao et al., 2020)

gsm8k_shaped_math.format: Problem -> step-by-step solution -> 'The answer is N' with final numerical answer
gsm8k_shaped_math.verification: Final numerical answer matches expected; reasoning steps sensible
gsm8k_shaped_math.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against GSM8K test set (1,319 test problems); numerical fingerprinting as additional check but not sole method

sources.name: GSM8K_train
sources.description: GSM8K public training split (Cobbe et al. 2021); 7,473 grade-school math word problems with full step-by-step solutions; sample 2,000 from training split only

sources.name: GSM8K_train_variants
sources.description: Variants of GSM8K training problems with number and context substitution (preserve problem structure, change surface details); generated programmatically

sources.name: MetaMathQA
sources.description: MetaMathQA dataset (Yu et al. 2023); GSM8K-derived problems with question rephrasing, self-verification, FOBAR, and answer augmentation

sources.name: ASDiv_and_easy_subset
sources.description: ASDiv dataset (Miao et al. 2020) easier subset plus single-step problems from GSM8K training filtered for ≤1 reasoning step; provides distribution breadth for easier problems

math_shaped.rationale: Base model weak here due to pruning; modest rehearsal to maintain not improve
math_shaped.teacher_rationale: Consistent with GSM8K rehearsal teacher; strong on LaTeX formatting and competition-style problems

difficulty_distribution.rationale: Skip mostly; model can't really do these

math_shaped.source: MATH benchmark training split (Hendrycks et al. 2021): 7,500 training problems available; sample 2,500 with overweighted lower difficulty
math_shaped.format: Problem -> LaTeX solution -> boxed final answer
math_shaped.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against MATH test set (5,000 test problems)

sources.name: MATH_train_levels_1_2
sources.description: MATH benchmark training split (Hendrycks et al. 2021) filtered to Levels 1-2 (easiest); covers algebra, prealgebra, intermediate algebra, geometry, number theory, counting/probability, precalculus

sources.name: MATH_train_level_3
sources.description: MATH training split Level 3 (intermediate difficulty)

sources.name: MATH_train_levels_4_5
sources.description: MATH training split Levels 4-5 (hardest); included sparingly because 3B model has limited capability at this difficulty

hellaswag_shaped.rationale: Base strong; modest rehearsal to maintain
hellaswag_shaped.teacher_rationale: Sufficient capability; cheaper than larger models

subcategories.name: hellaswag_style_completion
subcategories.source: HellaSwag training set (Zellers et al. 2019) and generated HellaSwag-style items in ActivityNet/WikiHow domains
subcategories.description: HellaSwag-format completion: 4-way multiple choice continuations of multi-sentence contexts; spans video-action contexts (ActivityNet Captions style) and instructional contexts (WikiHow style)

subcategories.name: commonsense_reasoning_broader
subcategories.source: PIQA training set (Bisk et al. 2020), SIQA training set (Sap et al. 2019)
subcategories.description: Broader commonsense reasoning: PIQA (physical interaction, 2-way choice), SIQA (social interaction, 3-way choice); extends commonsense capability beyond HellaSwag's specific format

subcategories.name: sentence_completion
subcategories.source: Story Cloze Test training set (Mostafazadeh et al. 2016)
subcategories.description: Story Cloze format: four-sentence story setup with two candidate fifth-sentence endings; pick the more plausible ending

hellaswag_shaped.format: Context (multi-sentence narrative or scenario) + multiple-choice continuation options (2 to 4 depending on source benchmark format); pick most plausible with brief reasoning
hellaswag_shaped.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against HellaSwag test set (10,003 test items); also against PIQA test, SIQA test, Story Cloze test sets

sources.name: HellaSwag_train
sources.description: HellaSwag public training split (Zellers et al. 2019); 4-way multiple-choice continuation of multi-sentence contexts spanning video-action and instructional domains

sources.name: Generated_hellaswag_style
sources.description: Generated HellaSwag-format items by Qwen 2.5 14B with ActivityNet-style and WikiHow-style prompts

sources.name: PIQA_train
sources.description: PIQA training set (Bisk et al. 2020); physical interaction QA, 2-way choice

sources.name: SIQA_train
sources.description: Social IQa training set (Sap et al. 2019); social interaction QA, 3-way choice

sources.name: Story_Cloze_train
sources.description: Story Cloze training set (Mostafazadeh et al. 2016); four-sentence story + two candidate endings

bfcl_shaped_tool_calling.rationale: Critical for agentic deployment (Aitomatic context); fragile to cultural FT
bfcl_shaped_tool_calling.teacher_rationale: Best format compliance for function call syntax

subcategories.name: simple_function_calling
subcategories.description: BFCL Simple category: single function provided, single call needed, correct argument types and values

subcategories.name: multiple_functions_one_needed
subcategories.description: BFCL Multiple category: multiple function definitions provided, model selects the correct one and calls it once

subcategories.name: parallel_calls_one_function
subcategories.description: BFCL Parallel category: single function provided, multiple parallel calls needed for the query

subcategories.name: parallel_multiple_functions
subcategories.description: BFCL Parallel Multiple category: multiple functions provided, multiple parallel calls across them

subcategories.name: live_realistic_apis
subcategories.description: BFCL Live category: real-world API definitions and user queries from production-like settings

subcategories.name: multi_turn_function_calling
subcategories.description: BFCL v3 Multi-turn category: extended conversational exchanges where model must retain contextual information across turns and execute function calls based on accumulated context; includes base multi-turn, missing function detection, missing parameter detection, long-context multi-turn, and composite scenarios

subcategories.name: error_handling_recovery
subcategories.description: Tool error responses and recovery patterns; recognizing failed calls and adjusting (covers BFCL v3 multi-turn miss_func, miss_param adjacent behaviors)

subcategories.name: relevance_refusal
subcategories.description: BFCL Relevance category: when no provided function is appropriate, the correct behavior is to not call any function

sources.name: xLAM
sources.description: Salesforce xLAM function-calling dataset (Liu et al. 2024); curated from APIGen pipeline; large-scale function call training data, primarily single-turn

sources.name: Glaive_Function_Calling_v2
sources.description: Glaive AI function-calling v2 dataset; diverse function definitions and call examples, includes some multi-turn

sources.name: Hermes_Function_Calling
sources.description: Nous Research Hermes function-calling dataset; instruction-tuned function-call format with reasoning

sources.name: BFCL_v3_multi_turn_generated
sources.description: Generated by Claude Sonnet 4.6 to match BFCL v3 multi-turn taxonomy: base multi-turn, missing function, missing parameter, long-context multi-turn, composite scenarios; covers the multi_turn_function_calling subcategory

sources.name: API_Bank_filtered
sources.description: API-Bank dataset (Li et al. 2023) filtered subset; multi-turn API-call dialogues with state changes

bfcl_shaped_tool_calling.format: Function definitions + user query (possibly with prior turns) -> valid function call(s) in BFCL format; multi-turn scenarios include conversation history and may require state tracking across turns
format_requirements: Output must parse as valid function call
bfcl_shaped_tool_calling.verification: Function call parses as valid JSON; function name exists in provided tools; args type-correct
bfcl_shaped_tool_calling.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against all BFCL test set subsets (Simple, Multiple, Parallel, Parallel Multiple, Live, Relevance, Multi-turn base, Multi-turn miss_func, Multi-turn miss_param, Multi-turn long_context, Multi-turn composite)

arc_challenge_shaped.rationale: Smaller benchmark but real; maintain via moderate rehearsal
arc_challenge_shaped.teacher_rationale: Strong general knowledge and reasoning at moderate cost; cleanly handles multiple-choice format without forcing verbose CoT

subcategories.name: arc_challenge_style_science
subcategories.source: ARC training set (Clark et al. 2018), drawing from both Challenge and Easy training splits; augmented with Qwen-generated ARC-style items
subcategories.description: Grade-school science questions in ARC Challenge style (harder subset requiring reasoning beyond keyword matching)

subcategories.name: openbookqa_style
subcategories.description: Elementary science questions requiring both core knowledge and broader reasoning, OpenBookQA format

subcategories.name: general_reasoning_mc
subcategories.source: Generated by Qwen 2.5 32B with diverse domain prompts
subcategories.description: Generated multiple-choice reasoning questions spanning science, logic, and general knowledge; complements ARC-specific items with broader reasoning

arc_challenge_shaped.format: Question + multiple-choice options (typically 4, sometimes 3 or 5) -> brief reasoning + answer choice
arc_challenge_shaped.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against ARC Challenge test set (1,172 items) and OpenBookQA test set

sources.name: ARC_Challenge_train
sources.description: ARC Challenge training split (Clark et al. 2018); grade-school science questions in the harder subset requiring reasoning beyond keyword matching

sources.name: ARC_Easy_train
sources.description: ARC Easy training split; easier science questions for distribution breadth

sources.name: OpenBookQA_train
sources.description: OpenBookQA training set (Mihaylov et al. 2018); elementary science with broader reasoning requirements

sources.name: Generated_reasoning_mc
sources.description: Generated multiple-choice reasoning questions by Qwen 2.5 32B spanning science, logic, and general knowledge

gpqa_hard_reasoning.rationale: Base model modest here; don't over-invest but maintain
gpqa_hard_reasoning.teacher: Kimi K2.6 (thinking mode) or DeepSeek-R1 - reasoning depth helps
gpqa_hard_reasoning.teacher_rationale: Only category where reasoning teacher genuinely adds value over instruction teacher

subcategories.name: graduate_level_science
subcategories.description: Graduate-level multiple-choice questions in physics, chemistry, and biology requiring domain expertise that competent non-experts cannot answer by quick search
subcategories.source: Generated by Kimi K2.6 or DeepSeek-R1 using GPQA-style prompts (Rein et al. 2023); no GPQA training set exists, so all items are generated

subcategories.name: multi_step_reasoning
subcategories.description: Multi-step logical reasoning problems beyond science: causal chains, structured deduction, complex word problems requiring sequential inference
subcategories.source: Generated by reasoning-mode teacher (K2.6 or DeepSeek-R1) with diverse multi-step prompts

gpqa_hard_reasoning.thinking_format: Use K2.6 native reasoning traces; preserve in <think> format where appropriate
gpqa_hard_reasoning.filtering_requirement: Discard examples where K2.6's reasoning contains self-correction artifacts (wait, that's wrong); keep only clean reasoning traces
gpqa_hard_reasoning.verification: Final answer matches expected; reasoning is internally consistent
gpqa_hard_reasoning.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against GPQA test set (448 items, including the Diamond subset of 198 items)

sources.name: Generated_gpqa_style_science
sources.description: Generated graduate-level multiple-choice questions in physics, chemistry, biology by Kimi K2.6 (thinking mode) or DeepSeek-R1, modeled on GPQA format (Rein et al. 2023); no GPQA training set exists, so all items are generated

sources.name: Generated_multi_step_reasoning
sources.description: Generated multi-step logical reasoning problems beyond science: causal chains, structured deduction, complex word problems requiring sequential inference

long_context.rationale: Long context preservation; needs context length variation
long_context.teachers_rationale: Qwen 2.5 14B is cost-effective for shorter contexts; Claude Sonnet 4.6 has strong long-context attention for medium/long contexts; teacher selection per subcategory by length

subcategories.name: short_long_context_2_4k
subcategories.description: Reading comprehension and QA over moderately long contexts (single article, conversation, or document)

subcategories.name: medium_long_context_4_8k
subcategories.description: Multi-document QA, longer article comprehension, or extended dialogue contexts

subcategories.name: long_context_8_16k
subcategories.description: Long-document analysis (book chapters, long reports); requires sustained attention across context

subcategories.name: very_long_16k_plus
subcategories.description: Stress-test contexts at and beyond model's full context window; primarily NIH-style retrieval tasks

sources.name: NarrativeQA
sources.description: QA over long stories and movie scripts; trains comprehension over narrative long contexts (Kočiský et al. 2018)

sources.name: QASPER
sources.description: Question answering over scientific paper text; trains comprehension over technical long contexts (Dasigi et al. 2021)

sources.name: HotpotQA_distractor
sources.description: Multi-hop QA with distractor paragraphs; trains attention across multiple sources (Yang et al. 2018)

sources.name: Long_Data_Collections
sources.description: Curated long-context training data including book QA and document QA; community-collected from multiple long-context resources

sources.name: NIH_style_synthetic
sources.description: Needle-in-a-haystack synthetic: place specific facts in long context, ask retrieval; generated with controlled needle placement

long_context.format: Long context + question -> answer grounded in context
long_context.verification: Answer grounded in provided context (LLM-judge check)
long_context.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against InfiniteBench, NIH multi-needle test items, NarrativeQA test, QASPER test, HotpotQA test sets

general_instruction_breadth.rationale: Distributional breadth from curated open-source instruction data; helps prevent narrow distribution effects from heavy benchmark-shaped data; supports general capability preservation indirectly.

sources.name: Tulu_3_SFT_high_quality
sources.description: Curated SFT mixture from AllenAI Tulu 3 (2024); broad single-turn instruction-following data; sample top-rated subset

sources.name: OpenHermes_2.5_curated
sources.description: Teknium's OpenHermes 2.5 (2024); diverse instruction data; quality-filtered subset removing low-quality and duplicated items

sources.name: OASST_top_rated
sources.description: OpenAssistant Conversations (Köpf et al. 2023) top-rated single-turn extractions; not full multi-turn (which would be measured elsewhere if it were a Meta benchmark, which it is not)

general_instruction_breadth.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against test sets of all evaluated benchmarks: MMLU, IFEval, GSM8K, MATH, HellaSwag, ARC Challenge, GPQA, BFCL, InfiniteBench, NIH, OpenRewrite
general_instruction_breadth.format: Open-ended instruction or multi-turn conversation -> helpful response in the style of the source dataset

code.target_benchmark: Not in Meta's official 3B eval but useful for deployment
code.rationale: Minimal preservation for code capability; not in Meta's 21 evaluation tasks but maintains baseline capability
code.teacher: Claude Sonnet 4.6 for generation and explanation tasks; existing curated datasets used as-is for CodeAlpaca and Magicoder

sources.name: CodeAlpaca
sources.description: CodeAlpaca-20k (sahil2801, 2023); instruction-style coding tasks with Python-heavy distribution

sources.name: Magicoder_Evol_Instruct
sources.description: Magicoder Evol-Instruct (ise-uiuc 2023); diversified coding instructions

code.verification: Code parses without syntax errors in stated language; for generation tasks, runs successfully on stated inputs when execution is feasible; for explanation tasks, explanation matches code behavior (LLM-judge check)
code.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against HumanEval (164 problems) and MBPP (974 problems) test sets
code.format: Coding task description (and sometimes existing code) -> code, explanation, fix, or refactor, depending on subcategory

subcategories.name: code_generation_basic
subcategories.description: Generate code from a natural language description; covers Python-heavy but multi-language

subcategories.name: code_understanding_explanation
subcategories.description: Explain what existing code does; brief technical Q&A

code.teacher_rationale: Claude Sonnet 4.6 produces clean, idiomatic code with good explanations; matches expected style for deployment

openrewrite_shaped.rationale: Small but in Meta's eval; often forgotten
openrewrite_shaped.teacher_rationale: Strong style and tone control; produces clean rewrites that preserve meaning; matches OpenRewrite's evaluation rubric for fidelity

subcategories.name: style_transfer
subcategories.description: Rewrite text in a different register (formal to casual, or casual to formal); preserve meaning while changing tone and word choice

subcategories.name: length_adjustment
subcategories.description: Rewrite text to a different target length (shorter or longer) while preserving the core message

subcategories.name: summarization
subcategories.description: Condense a longer text into a shorter summary preserving key information; varies in compression ratio

subcategories.name: paraphrasing
subcategories.description: Restate text in different words while preserving meaning; same length, different surface form

sources.name: Generated_from_diverse_texts
sources.description: Take diverse source texts (news articles, emails, technical writing, casual prose) and prompt Claude Sonnet 4.6 to produce rewrites in each subcategory style

openrewrite_shaped.decontamination_requirement: Exact match + 13-gram match + semantic similarity (>0.85) against OpenRewrite test set
openrewrite_shaped.format: Source text + transformation instruction (style, length, summarization, or paraphrasing) -> rewritten text that applies the transformation

conversation_regularization.target_benchmark: Not directly evaluated by Meta; included for anti-forgetting regularization
conversation_regularization.rationale: Cultural data is approximately 50% conversational in format (15% dialogue_completion + 35% direct_enactment, much of which is dialog-like). Without any conversational rehearsal, the model risks learning a spurious association between conversational/multi-turn format and Vietnamese cultural mode — i.e., the chat template itself becomes a 'cultural mode' trigger. This category is sized small and explicitly NOT cultural; its job is to break that association so the model treats multi-turn as a generic format that can carry any content. This is anti-mode-collapse regularization, not deployment-capability addition.
conversation_regularization.teacher: None (existing curated datasets used as-is); Claude Sonnet 4.6 for generated portion
conversation_regularization.teacher_rationale: Curated datasets like OASST, ShareGPT provide natural distribution; small generated portion fills specific anti-mode-collapse needs
conversation_regularization.design_principle: Content must be deliberately NON-cultural and non-Vietnamese. The goal is to teach 'multi-turn format does not equal cultural framing.' If the conversation content drifts toward Vietnamese cultural topics, it defeats the regularization purpose.

subcategories.name: generic_task_oriented_dialogue
subcategories.description: Task-oriented multi-turn dialogues on non-cultural topics: math help, code help, factual Q&A across turns, writing assistance; trains 'multi-turn is just a format'

subcategories.name: single_turn_chat_template
subcategories.description: Single-turn instructions delivered in chat template format (matching benchmark evaluation conditions); explicit anti-association data to ensure chat template doesn't become a cultural-mode trigger

subcategories.name: clarification_followup_generic
subcategories.description: Generic clarification and follow-up exchanges on non-cultural topics; model asks for missing info, user provides, model completes

subcategories.name: correction_revision_generic
subcategories.description: User points out error in non-cultural domain (math, code, fact), model corrects gracefully; trains correction handling outside cultural context

sources.name: OASST_multi_turn_non_cultural
sources.description: OpenAssistant Conversations (Köpf et al. 2023) multi-turn branches filtered to exclude Vietnamese, Vietnamese cultural topics, and broader Asian cultural topics; keeps task-oriented and generic helpful dialogues

sources.name: ShareGPT_filtered_non_cultural
sources.description: ShareGPT conversations filtered for quality and explicitly filtered to exclude Vietnamese cultural content

sources.name: WildChat_task_oriented
sources.description: WildChat (Zhao et al. 2024) subset filtered for task-oriented exchanges in non-cultural domains

sources.name: Generated_anti_modecollapse
sources.description: Generated by Claude Sonnet 4.6: explicitly designed multi-turn and single-turn-in-chat-template examples on non-cultural topics; targeted to balance the cultural data format distribution

conversation_regularization.format: Multi-turn dialogue or single-turn in chat template; content explicitly non-cultural; matches chat template tokenization of cultural data so model sees the template in non-cultural contexts
conversation_regularization.verification: Content filter pass: no Vietnamese cultural references, no East Asian cultural framing; conversation is coherent and task-completing (LLM-judge check)
conversation_regularization.decontamination_requirement: Not applicable for direct Meta benchmark contamination (not in 21 tasks); however filter out any items matching the cultural data corpus to ensure clean separation
conversation_regularization.anti_forgetting_specific_role: This category exists purely to prevent the model from learning 'chat template → cultural mode.' If this association forms, it would cause benchmark drops even though the benchmarks themselves are single-turn — because benchmark eval also uses chat template formatting.

decontamination_protocol.description: Mandatory before training; non-negotiable for valid evaluation

stages.name: exact_match
stages.description: Any rehearsal example with text matching test item (case-insensitive, whitespace-normalized) -> discard

stages.name: ngram_match
stages.description: Any rehearsal example sharing >13-gram with any test item -> discard. Standard threshold for catching paraphrases.

stages.name: semantic_similarity
stages.description: Embed all rehearsal and test items with sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Any pair with cosine similarity > 0.85 -> discard rehearsal example.

test_sets_to_check_against: MMLU-Pro (test set for the harder MMLU variant)
GPQA (448 test items, including Diamond subset of 198)
BFCL (all test subsets: Simple, Multiple, Parallel, Parallel Multiple, Live, Relevance, Multi-turn)
HotpotQA test set (distractor and fullwiki settings)
decontamination_protocol.buffer_generation: Generate 42K-45K candidates to get 40K clean post-decontamination

criteria_by_category.ifeval_shaped: Automated constraint check: does response satisfy the stated constraint(s)?
criteria_by_category.mmlu_shaped: Answer matches ground truth; reasoning (if present) is sensible and supports the chosen answer
criteria_by_category.gsm8k_shaped_math: Final numerical answer correct; reasoning steps are valid and lead to the answer
criteria_by_category.math_shaped: LaTeX final answer (in \boxed{}) matches expected; reasoning is mathematically valid
criteria_by_category.hellaswag_shaped: Picked option matches ground truth label
criteria_by_category.bfcl_shaped_tool_calling: Function call parses as valid JSON; function name exists in provided tools; argument types and values match schema
criteria_by_category.arc_challenge_shaped: Picked option matches ground truth label
criteria_by_category.gpqa_hard_reasoning: Final answer matches expected; reasoning trace is internally consistent without self-correction artifacts
criteria_by_category.long_context: Answer grounded in provided context (LLM-judge check verifies answer can be supported from context)
criteria_by_category.general_instruction_breadth: Source quality filter only (datasets are already curated; no re-verification)
criteria_by_category.code: Code parses without syntax errors in stated language; runs successfully when execution is feasible
criteria_by_category.openrewrite_shaped: Rewritten text preserves meaning while applying requested transformation (LLM-judge check)
criteria_by_category.conversation_regularization: Content filter: no Vietnamese cultural references, no East Asian cultural framing; conversation is coherent and task-completing on non-cultural topics (LLM-judge check)

length_distribution_target.description: Match cultural data length distribution to prevent mode collapse where 'long = cultural mode'
length_distribution_target.p95_long_tail: up to 2000 tokens for long-context category
length_distribution_target.validation: Compare rehearsal length distribution to cultural data length distribution before training; normalize if divergent

thinking_format_handling.description: Most categories use minimal/standard thinking; reasoning-heavy categories use extended thinking via K2.6 traces

mmlu_shaped.note: Brief reasoning helps; not all questions need extended

math_shaped.note: Harder problems benefit from extended reasoning

conversation_regularization.note: Conversational responses typically don't need extended thinking; match the natural distribution of generic assistant dialogue

thinking_format_handling.guidance: These percentages are estimates of natural teacher behavior. Let teachers (especially K2.6) decide reasoning depth contextually; do not force per-example.

teacher_cost_estimate.description: Estimated cost for generating full 40K rehearsal corpus. Includes anti-forgetting conversation_regularization subset which mixes curated and small generated portions.

pilot_validation.description: Critical step before scaling to full 40K
process: Generate 10% of each category's target volume proportionally (~4,000 total: 900 IFEval, 700 MMLU, 500 GSM8K, etc.)
Apply full decontamination and per-category verification
Fine-tune Llama 3.2 3B on rehearsal-only pilot (no cultural data) with this 4K corpus
Evaluate on all 10 relevant Meta benchmarks: MMLU, IFEval, GSM8K, MATH, HellaSwag, ARC Challenge, GPQA, BFCL, InfiniteBench/NIH, OpenRewrite
Compare to base Llama 3.2 3B Instruct scores
pilot_validation.success_criterion: Benchmarks stay flat or slightly improve; if any drops, the corresponding category pipeline has an issue
pilot_validation.decision_rule: Scale to full 40K only after pilot passes

expected_outcomes.description: Realistic capability preservation with this rehearsal structure + 20K cultural data at 2:1 ratio

stages.name: decontamination_setup
stages.description: Download and parse all evaluation test set items listed in decontamination_protocol. Build search infrastructure: exact-match lookup, 13-gram indexing, and embedding store for semantic similarity (sentence-transformers/all-MiniLM-L6-v2)

stages.name: pilot_generation
stages.description: Generate 10% of each category proportionally (~4,000 total) using the per-category teacher and category-specific prompts; apply full decontamination and verification on pilot output

stages.name: pilot_validation
stages.description: Fine-tune Llama 3.2 3B on rehearsal-only pilot; evaluate on all 10 Meta benchmarks; verify no benchmark drops significantly. If any category drops, debug that category's pipeline before proceeding

stages.name: full_generation
stages.description: Scale to full per-category volumes with buffer (~44K-45K candidates to compensate for expected 2-5% decontamination + verification filtering)

stages.name: full_decontamination_pass
stages.description: Run all three decontamination stages (exact match, 13-gram, semantic similarity) against every test set listed in decontamination_protocol; discard contaminated examples

stages.name: per_category_verification
stages.description: Apply category-specific verification criteria (constraint check for IFEval, answer match for MMLU/GSM8K, JSON parse for BFCL, etc.); discard failed examples

stages.name: length_distribution_normalization
stages.description: Compare rehearsal length distribution to cultural data; if median rehearsal length differs from cultural by >20%, trim long outliers or supplement short examples to match

stages.name: final_assembly
stages.description: Trim to 40K target by sampling from passed examples while maintaining per-category volume targets and source attribution; tag each example with teacher, source, and verification result

stages.name: final_decontamination_check
stages.description: Second-pass decontamination as safety check; this catches any items that slipped through Stage 5 due to indexing issues or new test set updates

quality_validation_metrics.decontamination_completeness: 100% of rehearsal examples checked against all relevant test sets
quality_validation_metrics.verification_pass_rate: Each category has documented pass rate; minimum 85% per category
quality_validation_metrics.teacher_attribution_documented: Each example tagged with generating teacher for traceability
quality_validation_metrics.length_distribution_match: Median rehearsal length within 20% of cultural data median
quality_validation_metrics.format_validity: All examples parse correctly in their target format (JSON for tool calls, multiple-choice for MMLU/HellaSwag/ARC, etc.)
